preamble.summary: The New Covenant. MT ch. 31 = LXX ch. 38. The New Covenant passage (vv. 31-34) is present in BOTH traditions — critically important for messianic theology.
preamble.notable_variants: LXX is slightly shorter in the surrounding material but the New Covenant passage (vv. 31-34) is substantially identical. Minor differences in the list of restoration promises.
preamble.structural_notes: MT ch. 31 = LXX ch. 38. The New Covenant (vv. 31-34) is the passage quoted in Hebrews 8:8-12.

The New Covenant oracle begins — present in both MT and LXX. 'Behold, days are coming, says the LORD, when I will cut a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah.'
The contrast with the Sinai covenant — present in both.
'I will put My law in their inward parts and write it upon their heart' — present in both.
'They shall all know Me' — present in both. This is the climax of the New Covenant promise.
